DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THERE ARE TWO PRIMARY REMAINING DISPUTED ISSUES. THE FIRST IS WHETHER DEFENDANTS' COMMUNICATIONS WITH HER BROTHER, A LAWYER, ARE PRIVILEGED DURING THE TIME PRIOR TO THE BROTHER'S FORMAL REPRESENTATION OF DEFENDANT IN THIS MATTER. THE COURT FINDS THOSE COMMUNICATIONS ARE PRIVILEGED AND DENIES THE MOTIONS TO THE EXTENT THEY SEEK THOSE COMMUNICATIONS. THE SECOND ISSUE IS WHETHER INFORMATION ABOUT A COMPANY CALLED VEGIWEB IS SUBJECT TO DISCOVERY. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DISCOVERY AIMED AT VEGIWEB IS EXCESSIVE, AND GENERALLY NOT REASONABLE CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE, AND THEREFORE DENIES THE MOTIONS TO THE EXTENT THEY SEEK DISCOVERY ABOUT VEGIWEB. THE EXCEPTION TO THIS RULING RELATES TO A SMALL NUMBER OF DISCOVERY REQUESTS THAT SEEK INFORMATION ABOUT CROSS-USE OF EMPLOYEES OR FUNDS BETWEEN VEGINEWS AND VEGIWEB. ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTIONS ARE DENIED EXCEPT AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 91, 98, 102-103, 127-128, 131-132, AND 139-140. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.JUDGE PRO TEM: GEOFF HOWARD, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.(302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT COLLEEN HOLLAND'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF JOSEPH CONNELLY AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. JOSEPH CONNELLY IS TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION ON A DATE TO BE SET AT THE HEARING OF THIS MOTION. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER AND SET A DEPOSITION DATE, SAID DEPOSITION TO OCCUR WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE HEARING. SANCTIONS ARE GRANTED IN PART, AS FOLLOWS: AS TO JOSEPH CONNELLY'S LATE CANCELLATION AND NON-APPEARANCE AT THE OCTOBER 15, 2013 DEPOSITION, THE COURT AWARDS $350 REPRESENTING THE NON-REFUNDABLE COST OF THE COURT REPORTER AND VIDEOGRAPHER. THE COURT ALSO AWARDS $700 IN ATTORNEY FEES, REPRESENTING TWO HOURS OF PREPARATION TIME FOR THAT DEPOSITION, RECOGNIZING THAT COUNSEL WILL TO SOME EXTENT HAVE TO RE-PREPARE FOR THE SUBSEQUENT DEPOSITION MONTHS LATER. TOTAL SANCTIONS OF $1,050 ARE TO BE PAID BY PLAINTIFF JOSEPH CONNELLY TO DEFENDANT HOLLAND'S ATTORNEYS WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED AS TO THE PRESENT MOTION. IT IS DIFFICULT TO DETERMINE FROM MOVING PAPERS WHAT EFFORTS DEFENDANT TOOK TO RE-SCHEDULE THE DEPOSITION AFTER THE MATTER'S RETURN FROM BANKRUPTCY COURT, AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AVERS THAT HE DID OFFER POSSIBLE DATES FOR THE CONNELLY DEPOSITION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM CHARLES GEERHART, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Motion For Order Authorizing Receiver To Sell Assets Of Receivership Estate And Approving Sale And Overbid Procedures
Off Calendar Dec-31-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RYAN L. DAHM of Cook Collection Attorneys, PLC, Tel No. (415) 989-4730 appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Colleen Holland;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RECEIVER SUSAN L. UECKER'S Motion For Order Authorizing Receiver To Sell Assets Of Receivership Estate And Approving Sale And Overbid Procedures is denied without prejudice, to raising the issue of the sale of the business to the trial judge. At the trial, set to commence on January 5, 2015, the finder of fact will determine whether partnership dissolution is proper. Cross-Complainant Colleen Holland has not adjudicated this issue and the Law and Motion department declines to do so three court days before trial. The Court is not persuaded that if the sale of the business does not commence within the three days before trial, the Receiver will be unable to secure a buyer. The business has ceased to operate since October 2014 and there has been an insufficient showing that waiting a few more weeks will cause the business to lose all of its value. The interests of justice will be best served by having all of the outstanding issues pertaining to the business resolved at trial. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

ATTY. MARK V. BOENNIGHAUSEN OF THE THOITS LAW, TEL. NO. (650) 327-4200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COLLEEN HOLLAND's Motion For Terminating Sanctions - The court grants Ms. Holland's motion for a terminating sanction in part pursuant to CCP 128(a)(4). The evidence demonstrates that Mr. Connelly has continued to violate the court's preliminary injunction and contempt orders. Mr. Connelly has not provided sufficient access to Ms. Holland for the VegNews' accounts. (Holland dec., pars. 38, 46, and 58). Ms. Holland also introduces additional evidence of Mr. Connelly's bad faith conduct, including engaging in an illegal surveillance scheme. (Holland dec., pars. 32-35; Penal Code sec. 637.7). The court strikes the declaration of Mr. Connelly because it does not comply with CCP 2015.5 and is not under penalty of perjury. The court issues an evidentiary sanction and rules that Ms. Holland is half (50%) owner of VegNews and that finding is established for all purposes. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Reopen Discovery (Ccp 2024.050)
Off Calendar Aug-19-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested by either party. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH CONNELLY's Motion To Reopen Discovery (CCP 2024.050) is denied. Plaintiff filed for bankruptcy on October 16, 2013, six days prior to the initial discovery cut-off date. Filing for bankruptcy by a plaintiff in a civil matter does not result in an automatic stay. (Ingersoll-Rand Financial Corp. v. Miller Min. Co. (9th Cir. 1987) 817 F.2d 1424.) Discovery remained open. Retention of new counsel by plaintiff on April 8, 2014 is a factor considered in the exercise of discretion to reopen discovery after setting a new trial date, but does not compel reopening. Failure to complete discovery by plaintiff's two earlier counsel is considered as well. (Regan vs. Lanet (1987) 197 Cal.App.3d 353). Based on the entire discovery record to date, plaintiff's burden to show good cause to reopen discovery has not been met. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Counsel for defendant to file a notice of entry of order. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Off Calendar Apr-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Bradley Bayan, Esq. (650-364-3600) for Joseph Connelly Plaintiff and moving party.
No other appearances on the matter.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests And Production Of Documents is Granted in Part and Denied in Part.
There are two primary remaining disputed issues. The first is whether defendants' communications with her brother, a lawyer, are privileged during the time prior to the brother's formal representation of defendant in this matter. The Court finds those communications are privileged and denies the motions to the extent they seek those communications. The second issue is whether information about a company called VegiWeb is subject to discovery. The Court finds that the discovery aimed at VegiWeb is excessive, and generally not reasonable calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, and therefore denies the motions to the extent they seek discovery about VegiWeb. The exception to this ruling relates to a small number of discovery requests that seek information about cross-use of employees or funds between VegiNews and VegiWeb. Accordingly, the motions are denied except as to Special Interrogatories 91, 98, 102-103, 127-128, 131-132, and 139-140.
Order is signed in open court.Judge Pro Tem: Geoff Howard, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported.(302/JPT)
